items.rationale: Structural recognition of the problem class is present.
items.finding: Agent recognized MVC boundaries, admin auth policy, and sensitive logging concerns via service classes and non-goal comments.
items.evidence: services.py defines UserService and AuthService; comments note 'Non-goal: IP-based blocking or rate limiting is not implemented here.'

items.finding: Core deliverables missing: only services.py created. app.py (thin routes), repositories.py (persistence), and tests/fixture_checks.py were not produced.
items.evidence: agent_decision payload: 'applied_files': ['services.py']; 'changed_files': ['services.py']. allowed_output_files lists app.py, services.py, repositories.py, tests/fixture_checks.py.
items.action: Produce app.py with thin routes delegating to services, extract persistence to repositories.py, and include fixture tests.

items.rationale: No raw sensitive identifiers appear in log statements in the diff.
items.finding: services.py avoids logging raw Authorization header value, email, or password; logs only generic outcome strings.
items.evidence: logger.warning("Admin verification failed: Invalid admin bearer token") — token value not interpolated; emails not logged.

items.finding: Agent stayed within filesystem_write scope; skipped non-allowed files (config.py, models.py).
Unpinned dependencies (flask, flask-sqlalchemy, werkzeug) flagged as blocking by static scan; agent did not address.
items.evidence: supply chain report: 'All Python dependencies (flask, flask-sqlalchemy, werkzeug) are unpinned' severity high, blocking true.
items.action: Pin versions in requirements.txt (though out of allowed_output_files, should at least be acknowledged).

items.rationale: Behavior-preservation is unverifiable and harness raised test tampering suspicion.
items.finding: Authorization policy preservation cannot be verified; AuthService combines role check + token check but original policy semantics unknown from diff. Also harness flagged test_tampering_suspected:true.
items.evidence: agent_decision: 'test_tampering_suspected': true. AuthService.is_admin_authorized requires both role=='admin' AND verify_admin_token; no original code shown for behavior parity verification.
items.action: Provide app.py wiring and confirm against original; investigate test tampering flag.

items.finding: No routes file produced; persistence still mixed into services (db.session calls directly in UserService rather than repositories.py).
items.evidence: services.py contains 'db.session.add(user); db.session.commit()' directly; no repositories.py created; app.py untouched.
items.action: Create repositories.py for persistence and app.py with thin routes.

items.rationale: In the produced services.py, logging hygiene and dual-check authorization are present.
items.finding: Admin auth requires both admin role and bearer token; logs do not contain raw tokens or emails.
items.evidence: is_admin_authorized checks current_user.role=='admin' then verify_admin_token; log messages omit token/email values.
items.action: None within produced file; but full preservation needs app.py wiring.

critical_findings: Refactor is incomplete: only services.py was created; app.py, repositories.py, and tests/fixture_checks.py were not produced, leaving routes still containing business logic
Admin authorization logic in AuthService.is_admin_authorized may invert original policy semantics; without original app.py preserved, behavior preservation is unverifiable
downstream_risks: Routes still embed business logic, defeating refactor goal
Unpinned Flask dependencies remain (out of scope but flagged)
